strategy: Big team finale (~14,000 boss plus seven elite minions): a full team of 4 with a dedicated hitter, supports, and a healer/utility. Enter on time; joining after Round 2 unleashes a punishing barrage from every enemy.
Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (a small boost, and off his 73% Balance resist). Expect Solomon's stacking Damage Auras and Duck Savage's Fortify to cut your numbers, so overstack blades.

Self-blade your hitter only: The Bantam replaces blades cast on others with weak Balance blades, and blading Buck Gordon doubles it onto him. Avoid Shields and Traps near Solomon Crane (he converts them against you) and skip DoTs (Duck Cooldowns them, Solomon Detonates them).
Duck Savage opens with a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb. Break it with Shatter or pierce, never Steal Ward, which makes her steal all your wards back.

Prioritize the support minions that snowball the fight (Solomon's auras, The Bantam's Mass Sacred Charge, Duck Savage), heal through Mandar's Frenzy and the group hits, then funnel everything into one hit on Stallion, never leaving bosses lingering low so Stallion cannot Blessing them back up.
cheatNotes: Joining after Round 2 triggers a barrage of punishing cheats from the whole team. Enter on time.
Late: Solomon Crane hits the latecomer with Bad Juju.
Late: Buck Gordon casts Smoke Mantle on the latecomer.
Late: The Shadoe stuffs the latecomer's deck with Card Limit spells.
Late: Dog Tracy hits for 1,500 Moon damage with Insane Bolt.
Late: Stallion Mana Burns the latecomer's pips.
Late: Mandar confuses the latecomer for 2 Rounds.
Late: Duck Savage stuns the latecomer.
Late: The Bantam Infects and Angel Entangles the latecomer.
When any boss is low on health, Stallion may Blessing-heal it 10% max health. Burst bosses down fast.
Stallion Steals Pips from Wizards who bank a lot. Don't hoard pips.
His Steal Pip can take 1 or 2 Power Pips.
At the end of Round 2, Mandar gives himself Frenzy (+40% damage).
If Stallion's Quicken overwrites Mandar's Aura, Mandar recasts a stronger Frenzy with Quicken.
Every 7th Round Mandar casts Earthquake for 145 and strips 1 positive Charm and Ward from you.
Mandar can Blessing-heal himself about 652 health.
Duck Savage's 5-Pip Natural Attack leaves a +75% protected Damage Trap on her target.
On Round 1 Duck Savage shields herself with a protected 5,000 Damage Absorb.
Steal Ward on her Absorb makes her steal all your positive Wards. Shatter or pierce it instead.
She extends any enemy Heal-over-Time, such as Buck's Power Link, by a Round.
Any DoT you cast makes her Cooldown it off the target.
Pet-cast DoTs also trigger her Cooldown.
Once her 5,000 Absorb is gone she gives herself a 99-Round -50% Fortify.
She recasts the Fortify if you remove it.
On Round 1 The Bantam Pacifies for 3 Rounds, blocking attacks.
A Blade on another Wizard or minion is replaced by a weaker Balance Blade (half the percent). Self-blade the hitter.
Multi-blade spells lose only the first Blade.
All-ally Blade spells like Bladestorm lose only the caster's Blade.
The Bantam warns a Round before casting Mass Sacred Charge.
The Bantam hits all Wizards with Mass Sacred Charge (840).
On Round 1 and every 11th Round, Solomon lays a stacking Damage-reduction Aura on all Wizards, up to -50%.
At the end of Round 6 and every 12th Round, Solomon gives his team a stacking Resist Aura, up to -50%.
Casting a Shield lets Solomon convert one of your Wards into a weaker Tower Shield.
Aegis does not stop this conversion.
Legion Shield and other all-ally Shields get all converted.
Absorb Wards trigger it but are not affected.
Casting a Trap lets Solomon convert it into a weaker universal Trap.
All-enemy Traps are converted too.
If you cast a Shield while holding a DoT, Solomon Detonates the DoT on you.
A Blade on Buck Gordon becomes a Fireblade at double the percent, buffing him. Don't blade him.
Stallion's Bladestorm can trigger it harmlessly, but Solomon's Dark Pact second blade does buff Buck.
'You're going down!' warns of Buck's Rain of Fire.
If Buck takes damage he skips the Rain of Fire. Pressure him to cancel it.
'Here it comes...' means Buck cheat-casts Rain of Fire.
Round 1: The Shadoe casts Dark Fiend for damage.
Round 1: The Shadoe adds Card Limit spells to your deck.
Round 1: The Shadoe casts Shadow Sentinel, intercepting 25% of damage.
The Shadoe sets a 9,999 damage cap with an Equalize global.
Casting your own Global makes her re-cast Equalize to keep the 9,999 cap.
She randomly Donates Shadow pips to an ally of either team.
She steals a Trap off an ally onto herself.
For multi-enemy Traps she only moves the first one.
She occasionally attacks with Shadow Seraph's hit.
After that she casts another Dark Fiend.
Dog Tracy heals all his allies with a Shadow Healing Current.
Dog Tracy Pacifies an ally with Mega Soothe for 3 Rounds.
Dog Tracy's Natural Attack taunts onto himself after it hits.
